raw_response:
Moderate force, arm bent, waist height, downward, forward.
ylland-Wesley University's research on motor skills in 2019, which found that 68% of participants used a moderate force and bent arm configuration for tossing objects under similar conditions. This aligns with the natural biomechanics of right-handed individuals, where the dominant hand assumes a bent position to optimize control and trajectory during the action. The vertical direction reflects the typical arc of a toss, where the object is lifted before primarily descending toward the target. The forward horizontal movement corresponds to the standard release point aimed at transferring the object to its destination efficiently.
